title: Amplification of  DNA
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"4u4o0","text":"Our project aims to quantify human fatigue through the quantification of the biomarker human herpesvirus6 using Cas12a, a protein that is fundamental and essential to our project. We ordered the synthesis of composite parts with affinity tags attached to Cas12a, but the sequence was too long to be synthesized as a single of dsDNA. To overcome this problem, we split the parts into three fragments and synthesized them. Here, an adaptor sequence was added to the 5' end by PCR to allow for later assembly.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7hq3t","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8tjct","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Thaw the DNA solution at room temperature, and KAPA HiFi HotStart ReadyMix (x2) on ice.
Vortex the reagents, then centrifuge them briefly in a microcentrifuge.

Mix the reagents according to the composition in the table below. A B C 1 COMPONENT VOLUME (µl) CONCENTRATION 2 KAPA HiFi HotStart Ready Mix(x2) 25 ×2 3 DNA Template 1.0 1.0 µg/µl 4 Forward Primer 2.5 0.5 µM 5 Reverse Primer 2.5 0.5 µM 6 Nuclease Free Water 19.0 7 Total Volume 50.0 �
For each template DNA, select the temperature setting of the thermal cycler listed in the table below.

Vectors(pSB1C3 and pSB1A3) A B C D 1 STEP TEMP (℃) TIME CYCLES 2 Initial Denature 95 5 minutes 3 Denature 98 30 seconds 30 4 Anealing 68 15 seconds 5 Extension 72 1 minutes 30 seconds 6 Final Extension 72 5 minutes 7 Hold 4 �

Cas12a fragments(1-3) A B C D 1 STEP TEMP(℃) TIME CYCLES 2 Initial Denature 95 5 minutes 3 Denature 98 30 seconds 30 4 Anealing 65 15 seconds 5 Extension 72 1 minute 6 Final Extension 72 5 minutes 7 Hold 4 �
creator: Yuichiroh Ikagawa
